fact: Allah challenged all of mankind and jinn to produce anything like the Qur'an — and none ever could.
The Prophet ﷺ pointed toward the moon, and Allah split it into two parts before the eyes of the Quraysh.
The Prophet ﷺ was taken by night from Masjid al-Haram all the way to Masjid al-Aqsa.
The Prophet ﷺ ascended through the seven heavens and met the other prophets, peace be upon them all.
The five daily prayers were given directly to the Prophet ﷺ during his ascension to the heavens.
Water flowed from between the fingers of the Prophet ﷺ until all the companions drank and made wudu.
The Prophet ﷺ placed his hand in a small vessel, and water began flowing from it for the companions.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ over a little water, and it became enough for a large group of people.
At Hudaybiyyah the companions were nearly out of water — the Prophet ﷺ blessed what remained and it sufficed for all.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ for rain, and rain began to fall before he even stepped down from the minbar.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ for the rain to stop, and the clouds parted and moved away from Madinah.
During the Battle of the Trench, the Prophet ﷺ fed many companions from a small amount of food.
The Prophet ﷺ blessed Jabir's small meal, and it ended up feeding a large gathering of companions.
The Prophet ﷺ blessed a small barley meal in Abu Talhah's house, and many people ate from it until full.
The Prophet ﷺ gave Abu Hurayrah a bowl of milk, and it became enough for the entire People of the Suffah.
Many companions drank from a small bowl of milk, and the Prophet ﷺ drank from it last after all of them.
The Prophet ﷺ blessed Jabir's dates, and they were enough to fully repay his father's large debt.
The Prophet ﷺ blessed a small amount of dates, and they continued to provide food long afterward.
The Prophet ﷺ touched ʿAli's painful eye before Khaybar, and Allah cured it immediately.
The Prophet ﷺ wiped over Salamah ibn al-Akwaʿ's wounded leg, and it was healed on the spot.
The Prophet ﷺ touched ʿAbdullah ibn ʿAtik's broken leg, and it was healed by Allah's permission.
The Prophet ﷺ wiped over a sick companion and made duʿāʾ for him, and Allah granted him healing.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ for Abu Hurayrah's memory, and he never forgot anything he heard from the Prophet ﷺ.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ for Anas ibn Malik, and Allah blessed him with great wealth, many children, and a long life.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ for Ibn ʿAbbas to deeply understand the religion and the Qur'an.
The Prophet ﷺ made duʿāʾ against certain enemies, and what he foretold happened to them exactly.
A palm-tree trunk wept and cried out when the Prophet ﷺ stopped leaning on it during the khutbah.
The Prophet ﷺ heard stones in Makkah greeting him with salām before the revelation had even begun.
Mount Uhud shook beneath them, and the Prophet ﷺ commanded it to be still — and it obeyed.
The Prophet ﷺ called for two trees to come together, and they moved toward him by Allah's permission.
After the trees came together as a witness for a man's shahādah, the Prophet ﷺ commanded them to return and they did.
A man raised a sword over the Prophet ﷺ to kill him and was struck with fear — the Prophet ﷺ remained unharmed.
Abu Jahl tried to harm the Prophet ﷺ while he was in prayer, but was stopped by a great terror.
At Khaybar, the Prophet ﷺ was warned about the poisoned sheep before it could harm him further.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that ʿAmmar ibn Yasir would be killed by a rebellious group — and it came to pass.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that his grandson Hasan would reconcile two great groups of Muslims.
The Prophet ﷺ told Fatimah she would be the first of his family to join him after death — and she was.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold the conquest of Jerusalem — and it happened in the time of ʿUmar ibn al-Khattab.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold a major plague would follow the conquest of Jerusalem — and the plague of Amwas came.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that wealth would become so abundant that people would struggle to find someone to accept charity.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that the treasures of Persia and Rome would be opened for the Muslims.
When Kisra tore the Prophet's ﷺ letter, the Prophet ﷺ foretold his kingdom would be torn apart — and it was.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that false prophets would appear after him — and more than thirty have come.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold the appearance of the Khawarij before they emerged, describing their signs precisely.
The Prophet ﷺ described the Khawarij in such detail that companions recognized them when they appeared.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that barefoot shepherds would compete with one another in building tall towers.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that trust would be lost and that leadership would eventually fall to the unqualified.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold a fire from the land of Hijaz that would illuminate the necks of camels in Busra — and it happened.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that his Ummah would follow the ways of previous nations, step by step.
The Prophet ﷺ foretold that his nation would divide and face trials, yet a group upon the truth would remain until the Day of Judgment.
